data.title: Apple Delays Siri AI Upgrade Indefinitely Due To Technical Issues
data.description:
According to PANews, Apple has indefinitely postponed the AI upgrade for Siri, originally scheduled for the iOS 18.4 update, with the personalized Siri feature now delayed until at least 2026. Insiders revealed that the project faced technical challenges, leading to potential reconsideration or complete overhaul.

Apple initially aimed to enhance Siri's AI capabilities to offer a ChatGPT-like conversational experience. However, poor performance during internal testing raised concerns among executives, including software chief Craig Federighi, about the feature's viability. Additionally, the upgrade may require more advanced AI computing power, potentially affecting compatibility with older devices and delaying future product releases.

Currently, Apple's AI team is under internal pressure, with employees questioning whether management adjustments are necessary. Meanwhile, competitors such as Amazon, Google, and Samsung are accelerating their AI technology integration efforts.

data.title: Michael Saylor Hints At Bitcoin Buying Opportunity
data.description: According to Foresight News, Strategy founder Michael Saylor recently tweeted a message that may suggest a potential opportunity to invest in Bitcoin. Saylor's tweet stated, 'If you have more than one chair (possibly representing funds or resources), this is your chance.' This statement is interpreted by some as a hint to consider purchasing Bitcoin during current market conditions.

data.title: Trump Acknowledges Potential Economic Challenges Amid Agenda Implementation
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, U.S. President Donald Trump has not ruled out the possibility of the U.S. economy contracting and entering a recession this year. He acknowledged that his comprehensive economic agenda might cause short-term disruptions but expressed confidence in its potential to drive future prosperity.

In an interview on Fox News' 'Sunday Morning Futures,' Trump was asked about the likelihood of an economic recession this year. He responded, "I hate to predict such things. There is a transition period because what we are doing is very significant. We are bringing wealth back to America, which is a big deal."

In contrast, U.S. Commerce Secretary Lutnick dismissed the possibility of an economic contraction during a television appearance last Sunday. Speaking on NBC's 'Meet the Press,' he stated, "The United States will not experience an economic recession. Since the onset of the pandemic in 2020, the U.S. economy has never fallen into recession."

data.title: U.S. President Trump Engages In Talks With Multiple Teams On TikTok
data.description: According to PANews, U.S. President Donald Trump announced that discussions are underway with four different teams regarding TikTok. He noted that all four teams are highly competent.

data.title: Singapore Exchange to List Open-Ended Bitcoin Futures Contracts
data.description: According to BlockBeats, market sources reported on March 10 that the Singapore Exchange is set to list open-ended Bitcoin futures contracts. This development marks a significant step in the expansion of cryptocurrency trading options on the exchange.

data.title: BBVA Expands into Crypto Trading
data.description: According to WatcherGuru: BBVA, Spain’s second-largest bank, has officially received regulatory clearance to facilitate Bitcoin (BTC) and Ethereum (ETH) trading for its clients.

data.title: Ethereum faces outage over Pectra upgrade on Sepolia testnet
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Ethereum's Pectra upgrade on the Sepolia test network is facing an interruption. An unknown attacker took advantage of the zero token transfer situation to mine empty blocks. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: BTC falls below 82,000 USDT
data.description: OKX market data shows that BTC fell below 82,000 USDT and is now at 81,996.6 USDT, a 24-hour drop of 3.94%. (This newsletter is generated with the assistance of AI)
